question: After birth, infant receives antibodies IgA through colostrum from mother. This is an example of ________ immunity.
question_latex: After birth, infant receives antibodies $IgA$ through colostrum from mother. This is an example of ________ immunity.
solution: Colostrum with $IgA$ is an example of natural acquired passive immunity as antibodies are naturally produced in mother's body and passive because the baby is receiving readymade antibodies.

question: The immune system is made of
question_latex: The immune system is made of
options: B. Humoral and cell mediated system
answer: B. Humoral and cell mediated system
solution: Acquired immunity is specialized immunity that forms antibodies and activated lymphocytes that attacks and destroys the specific organism. These are of two types, i.e., humoral immunity and cell mediated immunity. Humoral system involves antibodies and cell mediated system involves T-cells.

question: How does antigen-determinant differ from antigen binding site? [MHT CET 2009]
question_latex: How does antigen-determinant differ from antigen binding site? [MHT CET 2009]
solution: Antigenic determinants or epitopes are those sites on antigens, which are recognized by antibodies and receptors present on T and B-cells. Each antigen determinant can stimulate the formation of a particular kind of antibody or cell. The antibody's antigen binding sites are located on the variable portion of the antibody. These sites help antibodies to combine specifically with antigens in the manner of lock and key. Hence, antigen determinants differ from antigen binding sites by their location, function and structure also.

question: The letter T in T-lymphocyte refers to
question_latex: The letter T in T-lymphocyte refers to
solution: The letter T in T-lymphocytes refers to thymus. Thymus is haemopoietic as well as an endocrine gland. Thymus is the seedbed of thymic lymphocytes (T-lymphocytes). Certain stem cells, originating in yolk sac and liver in early embryo, but only in bone marrow in late embryo, migrate into the thymus and proliferate to form a large number of lymphocytes.

question: Interferons released by certain virus infected cells_______
question_latex: Interferons released by certain virus infected cells_______
options: B. Engulf and destroy invading microbes.
C. Kill most of the ingested micro-organisms
D. Make surrounding cells resistant to viral infection.
answer: D. Make surrounding cells resistant to viral infection.
solution: Interferons are special proteins released by virus infected cells. They also make the surrounding cells resistant to virus.

question: Antibacterial agent present in saliva to prevent infection is _____ [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Antibacterial agent present in saliva to prevent infection is _____ [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Saliva contains lysozyme which acts as an antibacterial agent that prevents infections.

question: In the cell mediated immuno response, T-lymphocytes divide and secrete
question_latex: In the cell mediated immuno response, T-lymphocytes divide and secrete
solution: In the cell mediated immune response, T-lymphocytes divide and secrete cytokines (Note: The PDF answer key states A - antigens, but the correct biological answer is cytokines. The answer as per the PDF answer key is recorded here).

question: Which of the following is NOT function of sensitized helper T-cells? [MHT-CET 2019]
question_latex: Which of the following is NOT function of sensitized helper T-cells? [MHT-CET 2019]
options: B. Production of perforins and cell killing substances
answer: B. Production of perforins and cell killing substances
solution: Production of perforins and cell killing substances is the function of cytotoxic T-cells, not helper T-cells.

question: Alteration of which genes leads to cancer?
question_latex: Alteration of which genes leads to cancer?
solution: Tumour suppressor gene normally keeps mitosis in check and prevents cancer from occurring. This gene must be inactivated, altered or removed to control cell cycle and prevent cancer.

question: Drug that binds to opioid receptors in CNS and acts as a depressant, slowing down body activities, is _____ [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Drug that binds to opioid receptors in CNS and acts as a depressant, slowing down body activities, is _____ [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: When opioids bind to opioid receptors, they trigger a cascade of chemical signals in the nervous system that reduce neuronal activity and lead to pain relief, feelings of pleasure and euphoria.

question: Aflatoxin and vinylchloride stimulate cancer of [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Aflatoxin and vinylchloride stimulate cancer of [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Aflatoxin (a metabolite of Aspergillus flavus, a mould) and Vinylchloride (VC) stimulate cancer of the liver.

question: Match the following diseases in column I with their respective modes of treatment in column II and select the correct option. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Match the following diseases in column I with their respective modes of treatment in column II and select the correct option. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Malaria - Combination of artesunate, sulfadoxine, pyrimethamine etc. (iii); Dermatophytosis - Drugs like nystatin, fluconazole, inraconazole (i); Amoebiasis - Drugs like metronidazole and tinidazole (ii); Filariasis - Diethyl-carbamazine citrate tablets (iv).

question: The person showing unpredictable moods, outbursts of emotions, quarrelsome behaviour and conflicts with others is suffering from
question_latex: The person showing unpredictable moods, outbursts of emotions, quarrelsome behaviour and conflicts with others is suffering from
solution: Schizophrenia is any of a group of severe mental disorders that have in common symptoms such as hallucinations, delusions, blunted emotions, disordered thinking and a withdrawal from reality. Schizophrenia can be caused by excessive dopamine production, alteration in neuropeptides, increased ventricular brain ratios and decreased frontal lobe activity.

question: Pathogenicity of Salmonella typhi is due to _______ present in its outer membrane. [MHT-CET 2019]
question_latex: Pathogenicity of \textit{Salmonella typhi} is due to _______ present in its outer membrane. [MHT-CET 2019]
solution: The pathogenicity of Salmonella typhi is due to lipopolysaccharides present in its outer membrane.

question: Bloating is a symptom seen in the patient suffering from _____ [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Bloating is a symptom seen in the patient suffering from _____ [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Bloating is a symptom seen in the patient suffering from diarrhea where absorption is affected and water is lost in defecation.

question: Which one of the following groups of drugs is called cannabinoids?
question_latex: Which one of the following groups of drugs is called cannabinoids?
solution: Marijuana, hashish, charas and ganja are called cannabinoids. They interact with receptors present in brain, obtained from inflorescence and other parts of Cannabis sativa.

question: Disease associated with secretion of toxin is
question_latex: Disease associated with secretion of toxin is
solution: Tetanus is caused by toxin from anaerobic, spore producing bacterium Clostridium tetani. It is also called lock-jaw disease because increasing muscle spasm makes opening of jaw difficult.

question: An autoimmune disease is [MHT CET 2008]
question_latex: An autoimmune disease is [MHT CET 2008]
solution: Myasthenia gravis is an autoimmune disease characterized by the chronic fatigability and weakness of muscles, especially in the face and throat, as a result of defect in the conduction of nerve impulses at the myoneural junction.

question: Treatment of malaria includes the use of ___ [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Treatment of malaria includes the use of ___ [MHT-CET 2023]
options: D. Combination therapies such as chemotherapy, radiation therapy, immunotherapy.
solution: Treatment of malaria includes the use of Quinine and Artemisinin based combination therapies (ACTs). WHO has recommended 5 different ACTs which includes various combinations of artesunate, sulfadoxine, pyrimethamine, etc.

question: Elephantiasis causing organism belong to
question_latex: Elephantiasis causing organism belong to
solution: The disease elephantiasis is caused by Wuchereria bancrofti, a member of phylum Aschelminthes. It is an endoparasite of humans that lives in the lymphatic system. The disease is marked by an excessive growth of connective tissue and enormous swelling of affected parts such as the scrotum, legs, arms and more rarely the vulva and breasts.

question: Match the diseases in Column I with the type of microbe causing them in Column II. Choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Match the diseases in Column I with the type of microbe causing them in Column II. Choose the correct option. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Elephantiasis - Nematode parasite; Typhoid - Gram-ve bacteria; Dermatophytosis - Fungus; Dengue - Virus; Malaria - Protest (Protozoa).

question: Natural acquired passive immunity is achieved by_____
question_latex: Natural acquired passive immunity is achieved by_____
options: A. Transfer of antibodies from maternal placenta to fetus
C. Injecting pre-prepared antibodies using serum from animals
D. Entry of pathogens into body
answer: A. Transfer of antibodies from maternal placenta to fetus
solution: Passive immunity is readymade antibodies obtained. Natural is produced within body due to stimulation. During embryonal growth, antibodies are produced by mother and transferred to embryo through placenta giving readymade immunity to the baby.

question: On sensitization, activated B-lymphocytes multiply to form clone of specific A which in turn produce B
question_latex: On sensitization, activated B-lymphocytes multiply to form clone of specific $A$ which in turn produce $B$
solution: Acquired immunity works in two phases - T-Lymphocytes (Cell mediated immunity) and B-Lymphocytes (Antibody mediated immunity). In antibody mediated immunity, lymphocytes produced by bone marrow are processed as B-lymphocytes which get differentiated into plasma cells which produce antibodies against antigen and memory cells which will remain in blood circulation and protect during next attack by same pathogen.

question: Which one of the following is NOT the symptom of malaria? [MHT-CET 2016]
question_latex: Which one of the following is NOT the symptom of malaria? [MHT-CET 2016]
solution: Dysentery is not a symptom of malaria. Symptoms of malaria include fever, shivering, and arthralgia (joint pain).

question: Carcinoma refers to
question_latex: Carcinoma refers to
options: A. Malignant tumour of the colon
B. Benign tumour of the connective tissue
C. Malignant tumour of the connective tissue
D. Malignant tumour of the skin or mucous membrane
answer: D. Malignant tumour of the skin or mucous membrane
solution: Carcinoma is a malignant or metastatic tumour. It can extend to the neighbouring cells; this process is called metastasis. These tumours are generally located in epithelial tissue and glands, e.g., breast cancer, skin cancer, stomach cancer, lung cancer, pancreas cancer, etc.

question: An antibody is described as bivalent because it has two ....... [2022]
question_latex: An antibody is described as bivalent because it has two ....... [2022]
solution: An antibody is described as bivalent because it has two antigen binding sites, one on each arm of the Y-shaped structure.

question: Consider the following four statements (I-IV) regarding kidney transplant and select the two correct ones. I. Even if a kidney transplant is proper the recipient may need to take immunosuppressants for a long time. II. The cell-mediated immune response is responsible for the graft rejection. III. The B-lymphocytes are responsible for rejection of the graft. IV. The acceptance or rejection of a kidney transplant depends on specific interferons. The two correct statements are
question_latex: Consider the following four statements (I-IV) regarding kidney transplant and select the two correct ones. I. Even if a kidney transplant is proper the recipient may need to take immunosuppressants for a long time. II. The cell-mediated immune response is responsible for the graft rejection. III. The B-lymphocytes are responsible for rejection of the graft. IV. The acceptance or rejection of a kidney transplant depends on specific interferons. The two correct statements are
solution: Both statements I and II are correct. The recipient needs immunosuppressants even after a successful transplant, and the cell-mediated immune response (T-cells) is responsible for graft rejection.

question: The polypeptide chains present in gamma immunoglobulin are
question_latex: The polypeptide chains present in gamma immunoglobulin are
solution: Immunoglobulin G (IgG) is the most abundant class of antibodies found in blood serum and lymph and active against bacteria, fungi, viruses and foreign particles. IgG possesses four polypeptide chains linked by disulphide bonds. (Note: Answer key states A-2, but biologically IgG has 4 polypeptide chains; answer recorded as per PDF.)

question: Given below are two statements. Statement-I: Passive immunity is acquired when readymade antibodies are received by body cells. Statement-II: Resistance induced by BCG vaccine is an example of passive immunity. Choose the correct answer. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Given below are two statements. Statement-I: Passive immunity is acquired when readymade antibodies are received by body cells. Statement-II: Resistance induced by BCG vaccine is an example of passive immunity. Choose the correct answer. [MHT-CET 2023]
options: A. Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
B. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
C. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
D. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
answer: D. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
solution: Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect. Resistance induced by BCG vaccine, polio vaccine is an example of artificial acquired active immunity, not passive immunity.

question: Match disorders in Column-I with their meaning in Column II. Choose the correct option given below. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Match disorders in Column-I with their meaning in Column II. Choose the correct option given below. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Amnesia - Loss of memory; Anorexia nervosa - Emotional aversion to food; Anxiety - Fear or apprehension; Bulimia - Extreme over indulgence in food.

question: Introduction of attenuated pathogens in human body results in _____ [MHT-CET 2017]
question_latex: Introduction of attenuated pathogens in human body results in _____ [MHT-CET 2017]
solution: Introduction of attenuated pathogens (vaccines) in human body results in artificial acquired active immunity as the body produces its own antibodies in response.

question: Accumulation of lymphatic fluid in testes, leading to their enlargement, is called _____ [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Accumulation of lymphatic fluid in testes, leading to their enlargement, is called _____ [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Accumulation of lymphatic fluid in testes, leading to their enlargement, is called hydrocoel.

question: The term antibiotic was coined by
question_latex: The term antibiotic was coined by
solution: The term antibiotics was first used by S A Waksman in 1945. Antibiotics are substances produced by microorganisms such as fungi or bacteria that are harmful to the growth of other microorganisms, e.g., penicillin, streptomycin, chloramphenicol, etc.

question: In order to prevent _____, an 'Rh-ve' Mother is injected with anti-Rh antibody during pregnancy, carrying 'Rh+ve' foetus. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: In order to prevent _____, an $Rh^{-ve}$ Mother is injected with anti-Rh antibody during pregnancy, carrying $Rh^{+ve}$ foetus. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: In order to prevent HDN (Haemolytic Disease of the Newborn), $Rh^{-ve}$ mother is injected with the anti-Rh antibody during all pregnancies carrying $Rh^{+ve}$ foetus.

question: BCG vaccine is used against
question_latex: BCG vaccine is used against
solution: BCG vaccine is used against TB (tuberculosis), which is a bacterial disease caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis. BCG vaccine was developed by Calmette and Guerin.

question: AIDS virus is also known as [MHT CET 2007]
question_latex: AIDS virus is also known as [MHT CET 2007]
solution: HIV is Human Immunodeficiency Virus, HTLV-III is Human T-Lymphotrophic Virus type-III, LAV is Lymphadenopathy Associated Virus. All are names of the AIDS virus.

question: Which of the following diseases is spread by housefly?
question_latex: Which of the following diseases is spread by housefly?
solution: Typhoid is caused by bacterium Salmonella typhi. The disease is spread by contaminated food and water through housefly.

question: Jaundice is a disease of
question_latex: Jaundice is a disease of
solution: Jaundice is a disease of the liver and involves yellow coloration of the skin, mucous membranes and secretions resulting from hyperbilirubinemia.

question: Syphilis is caused by_____
question_latex: Syphilis is caused by_____
options: A. A spirochete bacterium called Neisseria gonorrhoeae
B. A spirochete bacterium called Treponema palidum
C. A Diplococcus bacterium called Neisseria gonorrhoeae
D. A Diplococcus bacterium called Treponema palidum
answer: B. A spirochete bacterium called Treponema palidum
solution: Syphilis is a sexually transmitted disease mainly caused by spirochete bacterium Treponema pallidum. Symptoms observed are painless sore formation on the genitals, rectum and mouth. Second stage is rashes. Third and final stage can result in damage to brain, nerves, eyes and heart.

question: Interferons are class of cytokines to stimulate other cells against _____ infections. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Interferons are class of cytokines to stimulate other cells against _____ infections. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Interferons are a class of cytokines that stimulate other cells against viral infections as a part of innate immunity.

question: Natural killer cells that are important for nonspecific defense against viruses, are a type of _____ [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Natural killer cells that are important for nonspecific defense against viruses, are a type of _____ [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Natural killer cells (NK) that are important for nonspecific defense against viruses and tumors, are a type of lymphocytes.

question: Cannabinoids are obtained from [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Cannabinoids are obtained from [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Cannabinoids are obtained from inflorescences and other parts of Cannabis sativa. Marijuana, hashish, charas and ganja are different forms of drugs obtained from this plant.

question: Removal or absence of thymus in early life shall bring about
question_latex: Removal or absence of thymus in early life shall bring about
solution: Removal or absence of thymus in early life would result in lack of T-lymphocytes which are processed in thymus. The answer as per PDF answer key is C.

question: Diphtheria is caused by
question_latex: Diphtheria is caused by
options: A. Poisons released by living bacterial cells into the host tissues
B. Poisons released from dead bacterial cells into the host tissues
C. Poisons released by virus into host tissues
D. Excessive immune response by the host's body
answer: A. Poisons released by living bacterial cells into the host tissues
solution: Toxins released from Corynebacterium diphtheriae causes diphtheria. Actually, bacterial cells do not contain gene for toxin production - a phage carries the gene for it. Only those lysogenised cells of C. diphtheriae which carry phage can produce the toxin and cause diphtheria.

question: The genes concerned with the production of cancer are called
question_latex: The genes concerned with the production of cancer are called
solution: Genes that induce cellular proliferation are called proto-oncogenes. The proto-oncogenes encode for growth factors, growth factor receptors, transcription factors, etc. They change into oncogenes or cancer genes.

question: Cancer characterized by a change in wart or mole on the skin is called________
question_latex: Cancer characterized by a change in wart or mole on the skin is called________
solution: Melanoma is a type of cancer characterized by change in colour of wart or mole. Sarcoma is connective tissue cancer, carcinoma is epithelial tissue cancer, and adenoma is cancer of glands.

question: Antigen binding site in an antibody is found between
question_latex: Antigen binding site in an antibody is found between
options: C. One heavy and one light chain
D. Either between two light chains or between one heavy and one light chain depending upon the nature of antigen
answer: C. One heavy and one light chain
solution: A typical antibody monomer has four protein chains, i.e., two identical light (L) chains and two identical heavy (H) chains. The variable region from one light chain (L) and one heavy chain (H) form one antigen binding site. Hence, each antibody has two antigen binding sites.

question: Which of the following is a fungal disease?
question_latex: Which of the following is a fungal disease?
solution: Ringworm is a fungal disease. Ascariasis is a roundworm disease, malaria is a protozoan disease, and coryza is a bacterial infection.

question: Which of the following method is used for detection of antibodies?
question_latex: Which of the following method is used for detection of antibodies?
solution: ELISA test can be used to measure either antigen or antibody. For the detection of antibodies, following three assays are used: (i) Competitive ELISA (ii) Indirect or sandwich ELISA (iii) Antibody class captive ELISA.

question: Which of the following diseases are caused by the nematodes? A. Ringworm B. Ascariasis C. Elephantiasis D. Amoebiasis [2022]
question_latex: Which of the following diseases are caused by the nematodes? A. Ringworm B. Ascariasis C. Elephantiasis D. Amoebiasis [2022]
solution: Ascariasis (Ascaris lumbricoides) and Elephantiasis (Wuchereria bancrofti) are both caused by nematodes. Ringworm is a fungal disease and Amoebiasis is caused by protozoan Entamoeba histolytica.

question: Which of the following is an example of carcinoma?
question_latex: Which of the following is an example of carcinoma?
solution: Carcinoma is the cancer of epithelial cells that covers external body surfaces (skin) and the lining of body organs. While the cancer of muscle is sarcoma, cancer of lymph glands is lymphoma and cancer of blood cells is leukaemia.

question: An example of innate immunity is
question_latex: An example of innate immunity is
solution: Innate immunity is inherited by the organisms from their parents and protects it from birth throughout life. PMNL-neutrophils are an example of innate immunity.

question: Marijuana is obtained from _____ part of Cannabis sativa.
question_latex: Marijuana is obtained from _____ part of \textit{Cannabis sativa}.
solution: Marijuana is obtained from inflorescence of Cannabis. It is a psychotropic drug used primarily for medical and recreational purpose. It is considered one of the abused drugs. Dizziness, vomiting and hallucination are some symptoms.

question: Genetic material in human immune deficiency virus is _____ [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Genetic material in human immune deficiency virus is _____ [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: HIV is 100 to 140 nm in diameter. It is spherical. Virus particle shows centrally located two ss RNA molecules along with reverse transcriptase enzymes. It is covered by two layers of proteins.

question: The interferons are
question_latex: The interferons are
solution: Interferons are glycoproteins with low molecular weight that are effective against most of the viruses. These are naturally produced by virus infected cells. These proteins interact with adjacent cells and make them resistant to virus attack. Nowadays, interferons are also being manufactured through genetic engineering and are used in many antiviral drugs.

question: Which of the following diseases is not caused by virus?
question_latex: Which of the following diseases is not caused by virus?
solution: Tuberculosis (TB) is caused by a rod-shaped bacterium named Mycobacterium tuberculosis. It commonly affects the lungs. The bacteria damage tissues and release a toxin named tuberculin.

question: Fever due to infection is a natural defense mechanism of innate immunity which helps in the following EXCEPT _____ [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Fever due to infection is a natural defense mechanism of innate immunity which helps in the following EXCEPT _____ [MHT-CET 2023]
options: C. Providing specific immunity against specific antigens
D. Accelerating physiological processes to destroy invading pathogens
solution: Fever as a defense mechanism of innate immunity stimulates production of interferons, accelerates physiological processes to destroy pathogens, but does NOT provide specific immunity against specific antigens (that is acquired immunity) nor directly causes faster recovery.

question: With reference to the following diagram of an antibody, select the correct option indicating P, Q and R. [2022]
question_latex: With reference to the following diagram of an antibody, select the correct option indicating $P$, $Q$ and $R$. [2022]
options: A. P - Paratope, Q - Heavy chain, R - Variable region
B. P - Epitope, Q - Heavy chain, R - Variable region
C. P - Paratope, Q - Light chain, R - Constant region
D. P - Epitope, Q - Light chain, R - Constant region
answer: A. P - Paratope, Q - Heavy chain, R - Variable region
solution: In the antibody diagram, P indicates the paratope (antigen binding site), Q indicates the heavy chain, and R indicates the variable region.

question: Hallucinogenic properties are seen in____
question_latex: Hallucinogenic properties are seen in____
solution: Hallucinogenic properties are found in Atropa belladonna. Charas and ganja are found from Cannabis sativa. Papaver is poppy plant also used as medicinal plant. Claviceps is ergot fungus used to obtain LSD.

question: Quinine is obtained from
question_latex: Quinine is obtained from
solution: The drug quinine is obtained from the bark of Cinchona.

question: Which one of the following disease is sexually transmitted disease
question_latex: Which one of the following disease is sexually transmitted disease
solution: Syphilis is a sexually transmitted disease. It is caused by spirochaete bacterium Treponema pallidum. It affects the mucous membranes in genital, rectal and oral regions and causes lesions.

question: Given below are two statements. Statement-I: Tobacco, Marijuana and other drugs may cause infertility in both men and women. Statement-II: Nicotine blocks the production of sperms and decreases the size of testicles. Choose the correct answer. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Given below are two statements. Statement-I: Tobacco, Marijuana and other drugs may cause infertility in both men and women. Statement-II: Nicotine blocks the production of sperms and decreases the size of testicles. Choose the correct answer. [MHT-CET 2023]
options: A. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
B. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
C. Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
D. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
answer: D. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
solution: Both statement I and statement II are correct with respect to drug addiction and their effect on human body.

question: The truck drivers usually take [MHT CET 2007]
question_latex: The truck drivers usually take [MHT CET 2007]
solution: Amphetamines and cocaine are strong stimulants. Stimulants increase nerve activity in the brain by initiating the release of noradrenaline. Truck drivers use amphetamines to stay awake.

question: A non-communicable disease is
question_latex: A non-communicable disease is
solution: Diabetes mellitus is a non-communicable disease. It is characterized by chronic hyperglycemia, which is excessive concentration of glucose in the blood. Diabetes is primarily a result of relative or complete lack of insulin secretion by the beta-cells of islets of Langerhans in pancreas.

question: Which of the following is STD?
question_latex: Which of the following is STD?
solution: Trichomoniasis is a sexually transmitted disease. It is caused by Trichomonas vaginalis. Malaria is caused by Plasmodium sp. and Pneumonia is caused by Diplococcus pneumoniae.

question: Which of the following disorders can be regarded as madness?
question_latex: Which of the following disorders can be regarded as madness?
solution: Psychosis is mental illness characterized by loss of perceptions about realities of life. It is caused by brain disorders, TB, diabetes, hypertension, etc. The psychic is generally not aware of their illness and even refuses to undergo treatment; such persons are commonly called mad.

question: Given below are two statements. Statement-I: Innate immunity depends upon genetic make-up of an individual. Statement-II: The cough reflex is the only defense mechanism of respiratory tract. Select the correct answer from options given below. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Given below are two statements. Statement-I: Innate immunity depends upon genetic make-up of an individual. Statement-II: The cough reflex is the only defense mechanism of respiratory tract. Select the correct answer from options given below. [MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
B. Statement I is incorrect and statement II is correct.
C. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
D. Statement I is correct and statement II is incorrect.
answer: D. Statement I is correct and statement II is incorrect.
solution: Statement I is correct - innate immunity depends on genetic make-up. Statement II is incorrect - the cough reflex is not the only defense mechanism of respiratory tract; mucus, ciliary epithelium and nasal hair also help.

question: Identify the wrongly matched pair.
question_latex: Identify the wrongly matched pair.
solution: Plague is a bacterial disease. It is caused by a rod-shaped, non-motile bacillus, Pasteurella pestis. It is spread through the bite of infected rat flea (Xenopsylla cheopsis) on lower extremities.

question: The erythropoietic organ of foetus is _____ and _____ [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: The erythropoietic organ of foetus is _____ and _____ [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: The erythropoietic organ of foetus is liver and spleen. This function is taken up by bone marrow in adults.

question: Which of the following helps in differentiation of cells of the immune system
question_latex: Which of the following helps in differentiation of cells of the immune system
solution: The thymus gland secretes thymosin hormone, which stimulates the development of certain kinds of white blood cells involved in producing immunity.

question: Alcohol abuse or excessive use of alcohol during adolescence may have long term effects like _____ [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Alcohol abuse or excessive use of alcohol during adolescence may have long term effects like _____ [MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. Masculinization in females, excessive hair growth on face and body.
C. Increased muscle strength, improvement in overall performance.
D. Liver cirrhosis and pancreatitis, loss of balance.
answer: D. Liver cirrhosis and pancreatitis, loss of balance.
solution: Alcohol abuse or excessive use of alcohol during adolescence may have long term effects like liver cirrhosis and pancreatitis, and loss of balance.

question: Cells obtained from cancerous tumours are known as
question_latex: Cells obtained from cancerous tumours are known as
solution: Myeloma is a type of cancer that develops from plasma cells occurring in the bone marrow. It may develop wherever the plasma cells occur. (Answer as per PDF key is D - Monoclonal cells.)

question: The disease caused by virus, which is 42 nm in size and contains double stranded DNA is [MHT CET 2005]
question_latex: The disease caused by virus, which is 42 nm in size and contains double stranded DNA is [MHT CET 2005]
solution: Hepatitis-B virus contains double stranded DNA, while Hepatitis-C, Hepatitis-E, Hepatitis-A and HIV contain single stranded RNA.

question: Given below are two statements: Statement-I: Vaccines provide natural acquired active immunity. Statement-II: The latest hot spot in the field of vaccine research is the development of oral vaccines. Choose the most appropriate answer. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Given below are two statements: Statement-I: Vaccines provide natural acquired active immunity. Statement-II: The latest hot spot in the field of vaccine research is the development of oral vaccines. Choose the most appropriate answer. [MHT-CET 2024]
options: A. Both statement I and statement II are incorrect.
B. Both statement I and statement II are correct.
C. Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
D. Statement I is correct but statement II is incorrect.
answer: C. Statement I is incorrect but statement II is correct.
solution: Vaccines provide artificial acquired active immunity (not natural). The development of oral vaccines is indeed the latest hotspot in vaccine research.

question: A drug called morphine is obtained from
question_latex: A drug called morphine is obtained from
solution: The drug morphine is obtained from Papaver somniferum (poppy plant).

question: Select the INCORRECT pair with respect to substances produced by cells involved in immune response. [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Select the INCORRECT pair with respect to substances produced by cells involved in immune response. [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Suppressor T-cells suppress the entire immune system against attack on the body's own cells. They do not produce interferons. Interferons are produced by virus-infected cells.

question: The enzyme, present in saliva which acts as an antibacterial agent and prevents infections, is [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: The enzyme, present in saliva which acts as an antibacterial agent and prevents infections, is [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: The antibacterial substance Lysozyme (first described by A. Fleming) is a thermolabile, low molecular weight, basic protein which acts as a muramidase. Lysozyme is active only against some pathogenic Gram +ve bacteria.

question: The immunity developed by a child after attack of measles is __________ immunity [MHT-CET 2018]
question_latex: The immunity developed by a child after attack of measles is __________ immunity [MHT-CET 2018]
solution: When a child recovers from measles, they develop natural acquired active immunity as the body naturally encountered the pathogen and actively produced antibodies.

question: AIDS is caused by ______
question_latex: AIDS is caused by ______
solution: Acquired immune deficiency syndrome is mainly caused by Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV). Corona and rhino are types of cold virus; Nucleopolyhedrovirus causes disease in caterpillars in Australia.

question: ECG does NOT help to diagnose. [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: ECG does NOT help to diagnose. [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: ECG (Electrocardiogram) does NOT help to diagnose shortness of breath since it is a device to check the function of the heart, not respiratory parameters.

question: Which of the following is an opiate narcotic? [MHT CET 2011]
question_latex: Which of the following is an opiate narcotic? [MHT CET 2011]
solution: Morphine is a white crystalline, bitter narcotic principal alkaloid of opium. It is used in medicine as hydrochloride or sulphate to dull pain, for sedation and to induce sleep.

question: In the following diagram of Salmonella. Which antigens are indicated by x, y and z? [2022]
question_latex: In the following diagram of \textit{Salmonella}. Which antigens are indicated by $x$, $y$ and $z$? [2022]
solution: In the Salmonella diagram, x indicates the K antigen (capsular), y indicates the O antigen (somatic/cell wall), and z indicates the H antigen (flagellar).

question: Which one of the following correctly matches a Sexually Transmitted Disease (STD) with its pathogen? [MHT CET 2008]
question_latex: Which one of the following correctly matches a Sexually Transmitted Disease (STD) with its pathogen? [MHT CET 2008]
solution: Syphilis is correctly matched with Treponema pallidum. AIDS is caused by HIV; Gonorrhoea is caused by Neisseria gonorrhoeae; Anthrax is caused by Bacillus anthracis.

question: Which of the following provides immunity to digestive tract against antigen?
question_latex: Which of the following provides immunity to digestive tract against antigen?
solution: IgA is predominantly found in seromucous secretions such as saliva, tear, colostrum, milk. It is effective against a number of antigens, providing local immunity to the digestive tract.

question: In an antibody molecule, the antigen-binding site is present on_______
question_latex: In an antibody molecule, the antigen-binding site is present on_______
options: A. Variable regions of heavy and light chains.
B. The region holding arms and stem.
C. Constant regions of heavy and light chains.
D. Constant region of light chains.
answer: A. Variable regions of heavy and light chains.
solution: Two heavy and two light chains of polypeptide are held together by disulphide bond arranged in Y shape. The two arms of the fork consist of variable end with one heavy and one light chain acting as antigen binding site.

question: Which of the following options are CORRECT? [MHT-CET 2015] 1. Heroin - Stimulant  2. Marijuana - Cardiovascular  3. Cocaine - Hallucinations  4. Morphine - Sedative
question_latex: Which of the following options are CORRECT? [MHT-CET 2015] 1. Heroin - Stimulant 2. Marijuana - Cardiovascular 3. Cocaine - Hallucinations 4. Morphine - Sedative
solution: Heroin is a narcotic/depressant, not a stimulant (statement 1 is wrong). Marijuana affects the cardiovascular system (2 correct). Cocaine causes hallucinations (3 correct). Morphine is a sedative (4 correct).

question: Identify the wrong statements: 1. The tumour of haematopoietic cells is called leukaemia. 2. Cancer arising from the epithelial tissues of internal organs and glands is referred as melanoma. 3. Sarcoma is a type of cancer where bone and cartilages are involved. 4. Only benign tumours are called as true cancer or neoplasm.
question_latex: Identify the wrong statements: 1. The tumour of haematopoietic cells is called leukaemia. 2. Cancer arising from the epithelial tissues of internal organs and glands is referred as melanoma. 3. Sarcoma is a type of cancer where bone and cartilages are involved. 4. Only benign tumours are called as true cancer or neoplasm.
solution: Statement 2 is wrong - cancer arising from epithelial tissues of internal organs and glands is referred as carcinoma, not melanoma (melanoma is cancer of melanocytes). Statement 4 is wrong - benign tumours are NOT true cancers; malignant tumours are true cancers or neoplasms.

question: The causative agent of mad-cow disease is a
question_latex: The causative agent of mad-cow disease is a
solution: Prions are infective proteinaceous particles. This term was proposed by Prusiner. Prions cause some neurological diseases in animals (including humans), e.g., Mad cow disease in cattle and in humans it causes Kuru disease and Kreutzfeldt-Jacob disease.

question: Select INCORRECT match of type of drug and its effects on human body [MHT-CET 2023]
question_latex: Select INCORRECT match of type of drug and its effects on human body [MHT-CET 2023]
solution: Option A (Smack - causes extreme happiness, irritability and paranoia) is incorrect. Smack (heroin) is a depressant/narcotic that causes pain relief, sedation and euphoria, not extreme happiness and paranoia (those effects are associated with cocaine).

question: Harmful effect of cigarette smoking is
question_latex: Harmful effect of cigarette smoking is
solution: 95% cases of lung cancer are due to smoking. Benzopyrene present in tobacco smoke is carcinogenic.

question: Primary lesions called chancre are formed at the site of infection by____
question_latex: Primary lesions called chancre are formed at the site of infection by____
solution: Treponema pallidum causes syphilis, a sexually transmitted disease. Primary lesions (chancre) and secondary lesions are formed on different parts of body and genitals.

question: Penicillin was used in
question_latex: Penicillin was used in
solution: Penicillin was discovered by Alexander Fleming. It was the first anti-bacterial antibiotic, which is the product of Penicillium notatum and P. chrysogenum. It was widely used in Second World War.

question: Infection of amoebiasis occurs due to ingestion of ________ form or stage of parasite. [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Infection of amoebiasis occurs due to ingestion of ________ form or stage of parasite. [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: Amoebiasis is the disease caused by Entamoeba histolytica; infection spreads through faeco-oral route transmission. Infective cyst is ingested and pathogen starts its cycle in new host.

question: Which of the following is not hallucinogen?
question_latex: Which of the following is not hallucinogen?
solution: Hallucinogens include LSD, marijuana, mescaline, psilocybin, bhang, ganja, hashish, charas, etc. Narcotic drugs include opium and its derivatives such as morphine, codeine, heroin, etc. So heroin is a narcotic, not a hallucinogen.

question: Conjunctivitis is a symptom of ........ [2022]
question_latex: Conjunctivitis is a symptom of ........ [2022]
solution: Conjunctivitis (inflammation of the conjunctiva) is a symptom of acute coryza (common cold).

question: Consumption of which drug increases the concentration of dopamine in the brain? [MHT-CET 2024]
question_latex: Consumption of which drug increases the concentration of dopamine in the brain? [MHT-CET 2024]
solution: Cocaine is an alkaloid obtained from coca plant (Erythroxylum coca). It increases the level of neurotransmitter dopamine. Its excessive dosage causes extreme happiness, irritability, and paranoia.

question: Which of the following is a viral disease?
question_latex: Which of the following is a viral disease?
solution: Small pox is caused by DNA-containing variola virus (completely eradicated disease). TB is caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis (bacteria). Diphtheria is caused by Corynebacterium diphtheriae (bacteria).

question: Which group of drugs is normally used as medicine? [MHT-CET 2020]
question_latex: Which group of drugs is normally used as medicine? [MHT-CET 2020]
solution: Barbiturates are used to treat agitation, anxiety and insomnia. Amphetamines are used to treat depression. Benzodiazepines are used to relieve stress and anxiety, help people sleep, and can also treat alcohol withdrawal and epilepsy.

question: When a person dies after stung by bee followed by the administration of penicillin, death may be due to
question_latex: When a person dies after stung by bee followed by the administration of penicillin, death may be due to
solution: When a specific allergen (penicillin) is injected directly into the circulation, the allergen can react with basophils of the blood and mast cells if they have been sensitized by attachment of IgE region. This is called anaphylactic shock. The person dies of circulatory shock within a few minutes unless treated with epinephrine to oppose the effects of histamine.

question: Antibodies in our body are complex
question_latex: Antibodies in our body are complex
solution: Antibodies are proteins (glycoproteins) called immunoglobulins. These are produced by the lymphocytes in response to entry of a foreign substance or antigen into the body.
